title: Rain and sun shield for a covered golf cart
abstract: [0066] A rain and sun shield for a covered golf cart semi-permanently attached to the roof of a golf cart. The idevice is easy to install; will retrofit to any standard golf cart roof; does not interfere with the parking and storing profile of a fleet of golf carts. The device is easily opened and closed to provide the golf cart user with added sun and rain protection when in use.
claims: Claims CLAIMS WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A rain and sun shield device comprising: a pair of panels having a top and bottom surface, and a proximal and perimeter edge; with each of said pair of panels attached to at least one hinge; said hinge having a proximal and distal edge; said distal edge of each hinge is attached to the proximal edge of each panel and said proximal side of each hinge is attached to a golf cart roof, said roof having a top, an underside, a left and a right side edge; one of each of said pair of hinges is attached near the right side edge of the golf cart roof top; the other of each said pair of hinges is attached near the left side edge of the golf cart roof top; and whereby said hinges, the panels are deployed to unfold about 180 degrees to lie partly on the golf cart roof top and also extend beyond the right and left edges of the golf cart roof. 2. The device of claim 1 further comprising: a golf cart frame to which the golf cart roof is attached; and at least one pair of retaining lines each having two ends, whereby one end is attached to the rear edge of each deployed panel and the other end is temporarily attached to an anchor point located at the rear of said golf cart frame below the cart roof. 3. The device of claim 1 further comprising: a golf cart frame attached to the golf cart roof; and at least one pair of retaining lines each having two ends, whereby one end is attached to the front edge of each deployed panel and the other end is temporarily attached to an anchor point located on the front of said golf cart frame below the cart roof. 4. The device of claim 1 wherein the deployed panels lie partly on the golf cart roof and also extend beyond the right and left sides of the golf cart roof by up to about 24 inches. 5. The device of claim 1, further comprising a plurality of flexible grommets attached to each side of the panels that engage the top surface of the golf cart. 6. The device of claim 1, further comprising at least one clasp attached to the front edge of the golf cart roof and a mating clasp attached to the front edge of each panel. 7. The device of claim 1, wherein the hinge is semi-permanently attached to the golf cart roof using a mechanical fastener comprising screws or bolts. 8. The device of claim 1 , wherein the panels are lightweight waterproof material comprising plastic, high impact polyethylene, fiberglass or composite material and said panels are nested within the edges of the roof when undeployed. 9. A rain and sun shield device comprising: a main support rod, having an upper side and a lower side; said rod attached to a golf cart roof, said roof having a top and bottom and a front, back, right and left edge and said attachment of the rod to the roof is oriented front to back; a pair of two stacked panels; each pair of stacked panels having an upper and lower panel; each upper and lower panel having a top and bottom surface, and a proximal and perimeter edge; wherein the proximal end of each lower panel is attached to the main support rod and the perimeter edge of each lower panel is attached to the proximal edge of the upper panel by at least one hinge; and whereby said hinges, the upper panels are deployed to unfold about 180 degrees to lie partly on the golf cart roof top and also extend beyond the right and left edges of the golf cart roof. 10. The device of claim 9, further comprising: a golf cart frame attached to the bottom of the golf cart roof; and at least one pair of retaining lines each having two ends, whereby one end is attached to each upper panel and the other end is temporarily attached to an anchor point located on the golf cart frame below the cart roof. 11. The device of claim 9, further comprising a plurality of flexible grommets attached to each side of the panels that engage the top surface of the golf cart. 12. The device of claim 9, further comprising at least one clasp attached to the front edge of the golf cart roof and a mating clasp attached to the front edge of each upper panel. 13. The device of claim 9, wherein the panels are lightweight waterproof material comprising plastic, high impact polyethylene, fiberglass or composite material. 14. A method for enhancing rain and sun protection of a golf cart roof comprising: deploying a rain and sun shield device comprising: a pair of panels having a top and bottom surface, and a proximal and perimeter edge; with each of said pair of panels attached to at least one hinge; said hinge having a proximal and distal edge; said distal edge of each hinge is attached to the proximal edge of each panel and said proximal side of each hinge is attached to a golf cart roof, said roof having a top, an underside, a left and a right side edge; one of each of said pair of hinges is attached near the right side edge of the golf cart roof top; the other of each said pair of hinges is attached near the left side edge of the golf cart roof top; whereby said hinges, the panels are deployed to unfold about 180 degrees to lie partly on the golf cart roof top and also extend beyond the right and left edges of the golf cart roof. 15. A method for enhancing rain and sun protection of a golf cart roof comprising: deploying a rain and sun shield device comprising: a main support rod attached to a golf cart roof, said roof having a top and bottom and a front, back, right and left edge and said attachment of the rod to the roof is oriented front to back; a pair of two stacked panels; each pair of stacked panels having an upper and lower panel; each upper and lower panel having a top and bottom surface, and a proximal and perimeter edge; wherein the proximal end of each lower panel is attached to the main support rod and the perimeter edge of each lower panel is attached to the proximal edge of the upper panel by at least one hinge; and whereby said hinges, the upper panels are deployed to unfold about 180 degrees to lie partly on the golf cart roof top and also extend beyond the right and left edges of the golf cart roof.
